title: Settings
subtitle: Manage research preferences, model providers, local skills, and app updates here.

language.title: Interface language
language.description: Switch the UI and agent output language. Running sessions keep the language they started with; new sessions adopt the latest setting immediately.

research.title: Research preferences
research.description: These preferences are injected into the system prompt and take effect on the next new session; running sessions are not switched mid-flight.
research.loadError: Failed to load or save preferences: {{message}}
research.savedFlash: Saved. New sessions will pick up these preferences automatically.

purpose.title: Research goal
purpose.caption: Determines whether the Planner must use a causal identification strategy and how the Reviewer scores the work.

causal.label: Causal identification
causal.description: Explicit X → Y causal effect; requires identification strategies such as DID / IV / RDD / PSM.

associative.label: Associational exploration
associative.description: Correlations between variables; OLS / Logit / Probit with fixed effects or clustered controls are acceptable, with results clearly stated as associational.

webSearch.title: Web literature search
webSearch.caption: Controls whether the agent may search the web for references. Only literature search is restricted; data-source location and other web uses are unaffected.

on.label: Enabled (recommended)
on.description: The agent may search the web on demand for references, reviews, author pages, and similar literature material.

off.label: Disabled
off.description: Literature web search is off; literature-review uses only downloaded references and local resources. Non-literature uses such as data-source location and policy / API documentation are unaffected.

providers.title: Model providers
providers.subtitleEmpty: No model providers yet. Add one, or keep using environment variables.
providers.emptyHint: No model providers yet. Add one, or keep using environment variables.

preset.label: Quick-fill from preset
preset.hint: Selecting a preset will overwrite the ID, name, endpoint, model, and auth mode.

id.label: ID
id.hint: Unique identifier after saving; must not collide.
id.errorFormat: ID may only contain letters, digits, - and _

auth.label: Auth mode
auth.hint: Anthropic official typically uses api_key; third-party compatible endpoints typically use auth_token.

credential.label: API Key / Token
credential.errorEmpty: API Key / Token cannot be empty

autoCompact.label: Auto-compact threshold (optional)
autoCompact.hint: When cumulative tokens hit this value, the SDK auto-triggers compact. Leave blank to use a model-adaptive default: ~850k for 1M-window models, ~160k otherwise. Allowed range {{min}} - {{max}}.
autoCompact.errorRange: Auto-compact threshold must be between {{min}} and {{max}}

criticPanel.title: Review panel
criticPanel.description: An independent second perspective beyond the main model. Pick 1 for single-critic remarks, ≥ 2 to aggregate consensus and disagreement. Only anthropic-protocol providers are supported.
criticPanel.noProviders: No anthropic-protocol providers yet. Add a few different providers in the "Model providers" card above (e.g. Claude official + third-party compatible endpoint + your own proxy), then come back to configure the review panel.
criticPanel.warningNoneSelected: No review model selected. Pick at least 1 independent provider for an adversarial perspective.
criticPanel.savedSingle: 1 review model enabled (single-critic mode); /full-research and /review will dispatch it automatically. Add another provider to enter adversarial-consensus mode.
criticPanel.savedMulti: {{count}} review models enabled (adversarial-consensus mode); /full-research and /review will dispatch them in parallel and aggregate consensus and disagreement.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Skills installed by you. Import a `SKILL.md` file or a skill folder to install a new one.
skills.openDirTitle: Open the user skills folder in the file manager
skills.emptyHint: No user skills installed yet. Click "Import skill" to install one, or drop a `SKILL.md` into the user skills folder.

messages.manualLink: Open GitHub Releases to download the latest version manually
